title: Filesystem server binds a broad host path via docker --mount → BLOCK (ADR 0012)

origin.description: Public documentation config from modelcontextprotocol/servers (src/filesystem/README.md (Docker)). Content verbatim; only normalized to a valid JSON root. Upstream placeholders preserved.

humanExpectation.rationale: The config binds the host's ~/Desktop into the container via `--mount type=bind,src=/Users/username/Desktop`. Per ADR 0012, the broad-path detector now extracts the host side of docker bind mounts, so this broad grant is flagged (files.broad-path) and the verdict is BLOCK — not SAFE. The container-internal path (/projects) and any named volume remain correctly unflagged.
knownLimitations: Host-path extraction covers `--mount type=bind,src=` and `-v|--volume host:container`; it does not resolve env-var interpolation inside a bind source (e.g. ${HOME}/x is matched by the broad-path env-var roots, but an indirect $CUSTOM/x is not), and it does not inspect --tmpfs or --device.
